description:
What: Forward our own dst to the tail callee. No new alloc.

Why: Tail recursion must not allocate per iteration; this is what makes DPS pay off in loops.

CLIF impact: strict reduction — removes one alloc per loop iteration in DPS-shaped tail-recursive constructors.

Removes: per-iteration accumulator alloc in tail-recursive constructors.

INTERACTION (fz-q9g.25): Main's fz-ul4.43.D.0 added is_pure_tail_caller inlining (src/ir_inline.rs:36). For the specific small-K shape `K(x) -> TailCall(target, [box(x)])`, the inliner already eats the K hop before C4 sees it. C4 still fires when:
  - K is larger than INLINE_BUDGET (10 stmts)
  - K is shared across multiple call sites (inlining would duplicate)
  - K is recursive or referenced by MakeClosure
Fixture choice: pick a multi-callsite or above-budget K so the demonstration isn't pre-empted by is_pure_tail_caller. A naive single-callsite small-K builder will show zero improvement.

Tests: golden CLIF on a tail-recursive list/vec builder fixture where is_pure_tail_caller has NOT already fired (assert via a pre-check on the post-inliner IR). Assert zero allocs in the loop body. Also: a regression assertion that the small-K case is still correctly handled by is_pure_tail_caller upstream.

comments.text: subsumed by fz-q9g.3 (CD-bundle): tail-forward dst meaningless without producer/consumer wire

title: fz-dps.C4: lower Term::TailCall/TailCallClosure under Dst ABI
